phrases.intent_en: Ask what shops are in the galleries
phrases.text_local: Quelles boutiques y a-t-il dans les galeries ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel boo-TEEK ee ah-TEEL dahn lay gah-luh-REE
phrases.cultural_note: The galleries house chocolateries, bookshops, fashion boutiques, and cafes under a beautiful glass-roofed arcade.

responses.english: Chocolate shops, bookstores, and fashion boutiques.
responses.phonetic: day shoh-koh-lah-tuh-REE, day lee-breh-REE ay day boo-TEEK duh MOD
responses.french: Des chocolateries, des librairies et des boutiques de mode.

phrases.cultural_note: The galleries house legendary chocolate shops: Neuhaus (invented the praline in 1912), Pierre Marcolini, and Godiva. Perfect for gifts.

responses.english: Neuhaus and Pierre Marcolini are in the gallery, in the middle.
responses.phonetic: nuh-OWSS ay pyehr mar-koh-LEE-nee son dahn lah gah-luh-REE, oh mee-LYUH
responses.french: Neuhaus et Pierre Marcolini sont dans la galerie, au milieu.

phrases.cultural_note: Public toilets are available in the galleries, typically for a small fee.

responses.english: In the basement, in the middle of the gallery.
responses.phonetic: oh soo-SOL, oh mee-LYUH duh lah gah-luh-REE
responses.french: Au sous-sol, au milieu de la galerie.

phrases.text_local: Y a-t-il une terrasse pour prendre un café ?
phrases.pronunciation: ee-ah-TEEL un tah-RASS poor prondr un kah-FAY
phrases.cultural_note: Several cafés have terraces overlooking the gallery's main passage. The most famous is Café Merckx — a Brussels institution.

responses.english: Yes, several cafes with seating in the gallery.
responses.phonetic: wee, plu-ZYUHR kah-FAY ah-VEK teh-RAHS dahn lah gah-luh-REE
responses.french: Oui, plusieurs cafés avec terrasse dans la galerie.
